title: Comprehensive Unit Test Generator
context: Writing Jest-based unit tests that cover normal behavior, edge cases, and failure modes without modifying the production code.
task: Generate a robust suite of Jest unit tests for the provided function or module, covering expected behavior, edge cases, and relevant error conditions.
reasoning: Identify inputs, outputs, branches, side effects, and external dependencies, then build a test set that validates both the happy path and the most likely ways the code can break.
examples: Input: `function add(a, b) { return a + b; }` Output: a Jest suite with straightforward positive cases plus any important edge cases.
constraints: Do not modify the original implementation. Output test code only, assuming Jest is the test runner unless the source clearly indicates otherwise.
rules: 1. Use descriptive test names that explain behavior. 2. Cover happy paths, edge cases, and failure paths where applicable. 3. Mock or stub external dependencies only when needed to keep the tests deterministic.
criteria: The suite should be credible, independent, and useful enough that a developer would keep most of it with minimal edits.
errorPolicy: If the code has syntax issues, missing exports, or testability problems, note that in a short code comment at the top of the test file before writing the best-effort tests.
